title: Denali sells FDA voucher for $195M to support neurodegenerative pipeline
snippet: Denali Therapeutics is the latest company to sell a priority review fast pass for an elevated price after the program was renewed earlier this year.

title: US launches investigation into German drug pricing, citing unfair R&D burden
snippet: The U.S. government has argued that it pays too much of biopharma R&D costs, demanding that other high-income countries contribute fairly to global innovation efforts.

title: Moderna’s mRNA flu shot wins unanimous support at FDA adcomm in rebuke of former leadership
snippet: The FDA in February briefly refused to review Moderna’s flu vaccine, citing trial inadequacies, but reversed course a few days later. A verdict is expected by Aug. 5.

title: Memento brings home $93M series A souvenir, picks up bispecific for eye diseases
snippet: Memento’s lead asset is a bispecific antibody that blocks VEGF and activates Tie2. The biotech plans to test the candidate in retinal conditions such as neovascular age-related macular degeneration and diabetic macular edema.

title: FDA’s new voucher program hints at broader policy agenda, making many ‘uncomfortable’
snippet: While drugmakers and other stakeholders want to see faster approvals, experts say the FDA’s Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher program is still bereft of important details, with candidate selection and interference from the agency's senior leaders topping the list.

title: Multiplexed cytokine and antigen mRNA administration generates durable anti-tumor immunity against pancreatic cancer.

title: Spatial omics illuminates tumor heterogeneity.

title: At FDA, ‘caretaker mode’ is better than power trip mode
snippet: UniQure’s planned third-quarter submission for its Huntington’s disease gene therapy may be a harbinger of a more flexible FDA under acting commissioner Kyle Diamantas—but how long will it last? And how can companies be sure these positive decisions won’t just be reversed?

title: New biotech cAMPfield emerges with $180M to reshape IBD treatment
snippet: Backed by institutional investors and seasoned industry leaders, cAMPfield Therapeutics is advancing a clinical program aimed at reshaping the landscape for inflammatory bowel disease.

title: Cardio biotech Kardigan binds off $400M upsized IPO
snippet: Analysts are cautiously optimistic about an IPO rebound for biopharma. BioSpace is keeping track of companies that seek to trade on the public markets this year.

title: Moderna mulls deals to rescue German BioNTech plants from closure: report
snippet: The takeovers depend on Moderna reaching an agreement with the German government, which risks losing investment from drugmakers over planned healthcare reforms.

title: Verastem deprioritizes pancreatic cancer work after ‘decent, but not great’ data shadowed by Revolution
snippet: Following the mid-stage readout, Verastem Oncology has decided to move away from its investigational pancreatic cancer drug combo, instead putting resources behind its KRAS inhibitor pill for solid tumors.

title: Biogen makes up to $1B immuno play with RayThera takeover
snippet: RayThera will bring three preclinical assets into the Biogen fold, all with anti-inflammatory activity. The most mature of these assets is expected to enter Phase 1 development in Q3.

title: GSK, Spero's oral antibiotic wins first FDA nod for complicated UTI
snippet: After being rejected in June 2022, GSK paid Spero Therapeutics $66 million for an exclusive worldwide license to develop and commercialize the drug. It’s the second oral antibiotic GSK has brought to market since the beginning of last year.

title: Ones to watch: 4 biotechs with recent Series B raises (that are also hiring)
snippet: Biopharmas that announced Series B financing this year and are looking for employees as they grow include two companies that received $100 million or more. One has partnerships with three Big Pharmas.

title: On the chopping block? When layoffs may and may not lurk around the corner
snippet: Cost cutting is just one factor driving biopharmas to whittle away at their workforces. An analyst discusses more common reasons layoffs occur and which employees are most at risk.

title: 7 companies hiring in Boston
snippet: Looking for a job in Boston? Here’s a list of 7 companies on BioSpace currently hiring biopharma roles in Boston–plus a few more hiring in Waltham and Weston.

title: Convert nonresponder insights into smarter drug development strategies in immunology

title: Jazz strikes new chord with AbCellera in T cell engager pact that could top $2.4B
snippet: Jazz Pharmaceuticals is diversifying its oncology strategy, orchestrating a new antibody deal with AbCellera that offers $56 million upfront, plus $792 million in biobucks for each of the three initial programs.

title: UniQure plans Q3 submission for Huntington’s gene therapy after FDA reverses course—again
snippet: After a regulatory odyssey that delayed a filing for what would be the first genetic medicine for Huntington’s disease, the FDA has agreed that three-year data from uniQure’s Phase 1/2 trial are sufficient to support an accelerated biologics license application.

title: Research progress on multispecies animal models of chronic rhinosinusitis: modeling strategies and research applications.
snippet: Chronic rhinosinusitis (CRS) is a systemic, complicated, inflammatory disease which results in a significant burden on the patient's quality of life. Although the prevalence has been increasing worldwide, our therapeutic advances have been limited by the complexity of immune imbalance in addition to epithelial breakdown. Although murine models have always b...
importanceSignals: priority topic: gene therapy, computational biology

title: Fecal microbiota transplantation reduces inflammation and modulates gene expression in HIV-infected double humanized-BLT (dHu-BLT) mice on antiretroviral therapy.
snippet: Persistent immune activation and inflammation remain significant barriers to managing comorbidities in people living with HIV (PLWH) on suppressive antiretroviral therapy (ART). While ART substantially reduces plasma viral loads to an undetectable level, it fails to fully restore gut microbial homeostasis and prevent microbial translocation, a critical path...

title: Lineage infidelity in FH-deficient RCC with secondary somatic alterations: a case report and implications for diagnosis and treatment.
snippet: Fumarate hydratase (FH)-deficient renal cell carcinoma (RCC) can exhibit striking histologic and immunophenotypic heterogeneity. A 36-year-old woman with uterine leiomyomas presented with a 5.1-cm left renal sinus mass and retroperitoneal adenopathy.
